turns.name: JOHN G. ROBERTS, JR.
turns.text: Justice Alito has the opinion of the Court in Case 22-674, Campos-Chaves versus Garland and the related cases.

turns.name: SAMUEL A. ALITO, JR.
turns.text: We granted review in these cases and consolidated them to consider the circumstances in which an alien who is ordered removed in absentia can have that order rescinded. Each of the aliens involved in these cases was served with a notice to appear that failed to specify the time and place of the removal hearing but each was later served with a Notice of Hearing that provided the missing information. Nevertheless, each alien failed to show up at his removal hearing and was ordered removed in absentia. They argue before this Court that they may have their orders of removal rescinded under 8 U.S.C. Section 1229(a)(b)(5)(C)(2), which permits rescission "if the alien demonstrates that the alien did not receive notice in accordance with paragraph 1 or 2 of 8 U.S.C. 1229(a)," this is obviously a highly technical question and I doubt that anybody who did not read the briefs last night would benefit very much from an oral explanation of the Court’s reasoning. So I will simply say that for the reasons provided in our opinion, we hold that the aliens are not eligible for rescission under that provision. Accordingly, we affirm the judgement of the Fifth Circuit in Campos-Chaves versus Garland, reverse the judgement of the Ninth Circuit in Garland versus Mendez-Colín, and vacate the judgement of the Ninth Circuit in Garland versus Singh. Justice Jackson has filed a dissent that is joined by Justices Sotomayor, Kagan and Gorsuch.
